response.overview: Each year, about a million international students come to the U.S. to study, and roughly half try to stay after graduation. It often involves a lengthy and uncertain process.  Sugar in the Water explores the universal human experience of waiting through the lens of an independent filmmaker. Collaborating with twelve close friends and family members, the film unfolds in carefully chosen locations—indoors, outdoors, and within a studio setting—where their spontaneous actions reflect the subtle rhythms of anticipation and stillness. The result is an intimate meditation on time, presence, and the shared existential realities that arise when we simply wait.
response.title: Sugar in the Water
